LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT VALLEY OF CALIFORNIA, INC. DBA COLDWELL BANKER RESIDENTIAL BROKERAGE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT- THE DEMURRER TO THE SECOND, THIRD, TENTH, ELEVENTH AND TWELFTH CAUSES OF ACTION ON STANDING GROUNDS IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. UNDER THE FACTS AS CURRENTLY PLED, PLAINTIFF 2111-2115 BAKER STREET HOMEOWNER'S ASSOCIATION LACKS STANDING TO SUE. CIV. CODE SECTION 1368.3 DOES NOT CONFER STANDING BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NAMED THE OWNER OF THE UNIT AS AN INDIVIDUAL PLAINTIFF AND PLAINTIFF HOMEOWNER'S ASSOCIATION ASSIGNED ITS RIGHTS TO RECOVER ANY LEGAL DAMAGES RESULTING FROM CONSTRUCTION DEFECTS TO THE COMMON AREAS TO THE INDIVIDUAL PLAINTIFF. (COMPLAINT 9.) THE DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FAILURE TO STATE FACTS SUFFICIENT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFFS TO PLEAD, IF THEY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, THE EXISTENCE OF A CONTRACT BETWEEN PLAINTIFFS AND THIS DEFENDANT THAT WAS BREACHED. THE DEMURRER TO THE THIRD AND TWELFTH CAUSES OF ACTION ON STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS GROUNDS IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. AS CURRENTLY PLED, THESE CAUSES OF ACTION APPEAR TO ALLEGE WRONGFUL CONDUCT ARISING OUT OF DEFENDANT'S CIV. CODE SECTION 2079 DUTY TO INSPECT AND DISCLOSE. AS SUCH, THEY ARE TIME-BARRED. (SEE LOKEN V. CENTURY 21-AWARD PROPERTIES (1995) 36 CAL.APP.4TH 263, 273-274.) PLAINTIFFS ARE GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD A BREACH OR MISREPRESENTATION THAT DOES NOT ARISE FROM DEFENDANT'S CIV. CODE SECTION 2079 DUTY TO INSPECT AND DISCLOSE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT VALLEY OF CALIFORNIA, INC. DBA COLDWELL BANKER RESIDENTIAL BROKERAGE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT- The demurrer to the second, third, tenth, eleventh and twelfth causes of action on standing grounds is sustained with leave to amend. Under the facts as currently pled, Plaintiff 2111-2115 Baker Street Homeowner's Association lacks standing to sue. Civ. Code section 1368.3 does not confer standing because Plaintiff has named the owner of the unit as an individual Plaintiff and Plaintiff Homeowner's Association assigned its rights to recover any legal damages resulting from construction defects to the common areas to the individual Plaintiff. (Complaint 9.) The demurrer to the second cause of action for failure to state facts sufficient is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiffs to plead, if they can do so in good faith, the existence of a contract between Plaintiffs and this Defendant that was breached. The demurrer to the third and twelfth causes of action on statute of limitations grounds is sustained with leave to amend. As currently pled, these causes of action appear to allege wrongful conduct arising out of Defendant's Civ. Code section 2079 duty to inspect and disclose. As such, they are time-barred. (See Loken v. Century 21-Award Properties (1995) 36 Cal.App.4th 263, 273-274.) Plaintiffs are given leave to amend to plead a breach or misrepresentation that does not arise from Defendant's Civ. Code section 2079 duty to inspect and disclose. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
